message:
fix(grpc): malformed grpc-timeout header fails closed instead of falling back to server default

src/grpc/server.rs — A malformed grpc-timeout header value was falling
back to the server's default timeout, which is incorrect: a client that
sends an invalid timeout should not silently inherit the server default
(it could mask client-side bugs or allow timeout impersonation). Now:
Ascii metadata with an unparseable timeout returns None (no deadline),
Binary metadata returns None (not a valid grpc-timeout encoding), and
only None (absent header) falls back to default_timeout. Updates the
test from test_call_context_malformed_timeout_falls_back_to_default to
test_call_context_malformed_timeout_does_not_use_default, asserting
the deadline is None.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
